description: Notes from the human editor directing the curmudgeon to review specific items with specific focus areas. Check at the start of every run. Pending notes take priority over the normal tracker sequence. Mark notes as consumed after reviewing.

notes.context: Phase 2 rerun: re-evaluate cross-WIN / cross-section / prose-level integrity after ~6 weeks of substantive content changes since the original April 14 holistic pass. Goal is the structural / "step back from the weeds" assessment that no per-item review can produce — does the overall argument still build persuasively given everything that's landed since then? Are new cross-references missing? Are there new prose redundancies, tone drift, or structural inconsistencies introduced by recent expansions/rewrites? Find structural issues that no per-WIN or per-SEC review can catch.
notes.mechanism: All 9 entries in tracker.holistic_checks[] have been reset to status=pending and tagged with operator_phase2_rerun_commission. They will drain via your natural Priority 4 logic (one holistic per run when nothing higher-priority is in scope). At your current 12h cadence and the current empty pipeline (0 open issues, 0 queue), expect drain in ~4-5 days assuming no surge of change-driven work.
notes.consumption_instructions: Acknowledge this note on the FIRST holistic review of the rerun (whichever HOL pops first via Priority 4). Mark the note consumed at that point — do NOT wait until all 9 are done; further HOLs after the first are picked up naturally from the tracker and don't need note presence. After consumption, append the consumed record to human-notes-archive.jsonl per PROP-022 phase 2.
review_focus_hints: Cross-references between WINs: have new EXPs introduced cross-refs that should appear in other WINs but don't?
Narrative arc: does the Overview → Killshots → Per-WIN flow still build persuasively given new content?
Tone drift: have any recent rewrites pushed the tone in a way that's inconsistent with neighboring prose?
Redundancy: any places where multiple WINs/sections now say the same thing in different words?
Hierarchy: did any new prose elevate (or fail to elevate) something that should be in higher-level summary?
Stresstest: pick a fresh adversarial angle (something dome-adjacent that we don't currently address) and ask whether our argument can absorb it.
Taxonomy: are all the WIN/section categories still mutually-exclusive and collectively-exhaustive?
Missing: anything that should obviously be reviewed but isn't?
Completeness: per-WIN, do all the claims have evidence and verdicts? Spot-check ~10.
notes.note_about_two_previously_reviewed: HOL-CROSSREF was last at cycle 2 (status flipped to pending; target_cycle_for_rerun=3). HOL-COMPLETENESS was last at cycle 2 (same; target_cycle_for_rerun=3). The other 7 are still at cycle 1 and will produce their cycle-2 review.
